title: Native image I/O — replace deps/stb with platform APIs (macOS coordinator + Linux + Windows)
goal: Establish the Eshkol-side image-I/O contract that the three per-platform implementation tasks (ESH-0007 macOS, ESH-0008 Linux, ESH-0009 Windows) will conform to. Defines the C API (image_read, image_write, image_resize, image_format), the tensor shape conventions (HWC vs CHW), the supported formats (PNG, JPEG, BMP, WebP), the error reporting model, and the CMake find_package() integration points. After this lands, the three platform tasks are unblocked.
next_actions: Define inc/eshkol/core/image_io.h with the public C API: eshkol_image_read(path, *width, *height, *channels) returning a uint8_t* + format enum; eshkol_image_write(path, data, w, h, ch, format); eshkol_image_resize(src, src_w, src_h, dst_w, dst_h, ch).
Define lib/core/image_io_platform.h with a dispatch table populated by each platform implementation: { read, write, resize }.
Convert lib/core/image_io.c to delegate to the platform table, removing every #include of deps/stb/*.
Add cmake/FindImageIO.cmake selecting between platform backends: APPLE → CoreGraphics/ImageIO framework, LINUX → pkg-config libpng + libjpeg + libwebp, WIN32 → GDI+ (Gdiplus.lib).
Document the contract in docs/breakdown/IMAGE_IO.md: shape conventions, format support matrix, error reporting.
Keep deps/stb/ as a fallback ONLY behind -DESHKOL_USE_STB_FALLBACK=ON (gated, defaults OFF) so we can A/B during the rollout.
acceptance: lib/core/image_io.c contains zero references to deps/stb/.
CMake build succeeds on macOS, Linux, Windows with platform backend auto-detected.
Existing image_io tests in tests/v1_2_edge_cases pass without modification on the platform where their implementation lands.
ICC `native_image_io_no_stb` smoke probe flips from FAIL to PASS.
ux_payoff: Removes a vendored 17kLOC third-party dependency. Once all three per-platform tasks land, deps/stb/ can be deleted entirely.
